response.justification: Candidate has strong senior-level engineering experience and notable geospatial/GIS background (GISCollective, Green Map, FOSS4G talk), which aligns with the navigation/geo domain. However, the core technical requirement of modern C++ or Rust is weakly met: C/C++ work at National Instruments was ~10 years ago and brief, with no Rust experience. Recent stack is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Worth a screening conversation only if DLang systems work and GIS experience are considered transferable, otherwise a pass.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS platform experience (GISCollective, Open Green Map, FOSS4G speaker)
Senior-level systems work including low-latency, high-throughput services in DLang at Sociomantic
Linux, async I/O, and performance-oriented backend experience relevant to data pipelines
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months at NI, 2014–2015); no evidence of recent commercial C++
No demonstrated work on navigation/routing systems, routing tiles, or large-scale distributed-system fault-tolerance
